title: Johnstonella inaequata
intro: Johnstonella inaequata, also known as White Fendlerbush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its small white or purple flowers and narrow, elliptical leaves. Traditionally, Johnstonella inaequata has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Johnstonella inaequata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Johnstonella inaequata is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Johnstonella inaequata is classified in the family Boraginaceae. This species belongs to the genus Johnstonella, which is a group of flowering plants. The family Boraginaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of flowering plants. The genus Johnstonella is a relatively small group of plants, with Johnstonella inaequata being one of the species within it. Johnstonella inaequata is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
